title: poa palustris var. muralis
intro: Poa palustris var. muralis, also known as Fescue Grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. The herbal preparations of Fescue Grass include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the dried plant material in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
